audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Jericho siege, herem, Rahab rescue, and rebuilding-curse narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for closed city, seven-day procession, ram-horn trumpets, shouted collapse, total-destruction command, Rahab's household sanctuary, treasury metals, city burning, and curse on rebuilding.

verses.restored: Jericho was shut and tightly closed because of children of Israel; none went out and none came in.
YHWH said to Joshua, See, I have given into your hand Jericho and its king, mighty men of strength.
You shall circle city, all men of war, going around city once; thus you shall do six days.
Seven priests shall carry seven trumpets of rams' horns before ark; on seventh day you shall circle city seven times, and priests shall blow trumpets.

It shall be, when they make a long blast with ram's horn, when you hear sound of trumpet, all people shall shout a great shout, and wall of city shall fall under itself, and people shall go up, each man straight before him.
Joshua son of Nun called priests and said to them, Carry ark of covenant, and seven priests shall carry seven trumpets of rams' horns before ark of YHWH.
He said to people, Pass on and circle city, and armed force shall pass before ark of YHWH.
It was, when Joshua spoke to people, seven priests carrying seven trumpets of rams' horns before YHWH passed on and blew trumpets, and ark of covenant of YHWH went after them.
Armed force went before priests blowing trumpets, and rear guard went after ark, going and blowing trumpets.
Joshua commanded people, saying, You shall not shout or make your voice heard, and no word shall go out from your mouth until day I say to you, Shout; then you shall shout.
Ark of YHWH circled city, going around once; then they came into camp and lodged in camp.
Joshua rose early in morning, and priests carried ark of YHWH.

Seven priests carrying seven trumpets of rams' horns before ark of YHWH went continually and blew trumpets; armed force went before them and rear guard went after ark of YHWH, going and blowing trumpets.
They circled city on second day once and returned to camp; thus they did six days.
It was on seventh day that they rose early at dawning of morning and circled city in this manner seven times; only on that day they circled city seven times.
It was at seventh time, when priests blew trumpets, Joshua said to people, Shout, for YHWH has given you city.
The city shall be devoted to destruction, it and all in it, to YHWH; only Rahab the prostitute/sex worker shall live, she and all who are with her in house, because she hid messengers whom we sent.
Only you, guard yourselves from devoted thing, lest you devote and take from devoted thing and make camp of Israel devoted and trouble it.
All silver and gold and vessels of bronze and iron are holy to YHWH; into treasury of YHWH they shall come.

The people shouted and trumpets were blown. It was, when people heard sound of trumpet and people shouted a great shout, that wall fell under itself, and people went up into city, each man straight before him, and captured city.
They devoted to destruction all that was in city, from man to woman, from young to old, even ox, sheep, and donkey, by edge of sword.
To two men who spied land Joshua said, Go into house of woman, the prostitute/sex worker, and bring out from there the woman and all belonging to her, as you swore to her.
The young men, spies, went in and brought out Rahab, her father, her mother, her brothers, and all belonging to her; all her clans they brought out, and they placed them outside camp of Israel.
The city and all in it they burned with fire; only silver and gold and vessels of bronze and iron they gave to treasury of house of YHWH.

Rahab the prostitute/sex worker and house of her father and all belonging to her Joshua preserved alive; she has dwelt in midst of Israel to this day, because she hid messengers Joshua sent to spy out Jericho.

Joshua made them swear at that time, saying, Cursed before YHWH is man who rises and rebuilds this city, Jericho. With his firstborn he shall lay its foundation, and with his youngest he shall set up its gates.
YHWH was with Joshua, and his fame was in all land.

translation_notes: Jericho is presented as a sealed civilian city under siege, not merely a military outpost.
The seven-day procession combines liturgical numbers, ark, trumpets, silence, and shout; archaeology and chronology concerning Jericho remain heavily debated and must not be overstated.
Herem in verses 17 and 21 means devotion to destruction. The text explicitly includes men, women, children, older people, and animals, and must never be sanitized as a bloodless victory.
Rahab and her extended household are spared because of the sworn covenant. Their rescue does not morally compensate for or justify the killing of everyone else.
Silver, gold, bronze, and iron are transferred to YHWH's treasury while living beings are destroyed, exposing the morally disturbing economics of sacred war.
Rahab's continued identification by occupation must not stigmatize sex workers or diminish her covenant agency.
The rebuilding curse places the builder's children into the cost of urban restoration and must not be used as a magical threat or justification for harm to descendants.
The chapter's fame-and-presence conclusion reports the victor's theology and does not erase the dead of Jericho.
safety_note: Never use Joshua 6 to justify genocide, child killing, religious war, siege against civilians, destruction of animals, looting under sacred language, curses against descendants, or stigma toward sex workers. Preserve the source under categorical moral protest, keep Jericho's victims visible, and honor Rahab's rescue without turning one sanctuary into an excuse for mass death.
